metadata.title: A dataset for Chinese-English Terminology of Chinese Medicine

versions.WHO: WHO International Standard Terminologies on Traditional Medicine in the Western Pacific Region
versions.PMPH: English Terminology of Traditional Chinese Medicine (Internal Draft)
versions.WFCMS: International Standard Chinese-English Basic Nomenclature of Chinese Medicine

terms.englishExplanation: determination of the nature of a diseased condition; identification of a disease by careful investigation of its symptoms and history; also, the opinion (formally stated) resulting from such investigation

versions.PMPH: center cold (cold in the middle jiao)

terms.englishExplanation: attack of cold directly to the stomach and intestines
ancient term for neurosis characterized by mental strain, listlessness, sleeplessness, anorexia, pseudo heat and pseudo cold, bitterness in the mouth, yellow urine and rapid pulse
an acute disease characterized by sudden fainting
a pathological change characterized by sudden onset of diarrhea with profuse discharge

a group of diseases caused by the invasion of wind, cold, dampness or heat pathogen on the meridian/ channel involving muscles, sinews, bones and joints, manifested by local pain, soreness, heaviness, or hotness, and even articular swelling, stiffness and deformities, also referring to arthralgia

versions.PMPH: visceral syncope (syncope due to kidney yang debilitation)

terms.englishExplanation: syncope due to yang debilitation of internal organs
a type of impediment disease mainly involving the intestines
diseases characterized by marked decline of cognitive and intellectual functions, usually progressive, without loss of perception or consciousness
a warm disease contracted in spring
a diseased condition arising when the gallbladder qi is stagnant, and manifested by recurrent right upper abdominal pain with stuffiness and distension
a psychotic disorder characterized by severe depression

versions.PMPH: latent summerheat occurring in the winter
versions.WFCMS: disease of latent summerheat in winter

terms.englishExplanation: diarrhea after eating, with undigested food in the stool
cough due to ascending counterflow of lung qi
a consumptive disease due to overstrain, characterized by cough, hemoptysis, tidal fever, night sweating and emaciation
a disease of the lung due to chronic cough, marked by atrophy of the lung with shortness of breath and expectoration
abscess occurring in the lung due to heat toxin, marked by sudden onset of fever, cough, chest pain, expectoration of foul-smelling bloody purulent sputum
a disease of the lung characterized by persistent distension of the lung, manifested by a sensation of

versions.PMPH: wind syncope (syncope due to endogenous liver wind)

terms.englishExplanation: syncope due to transformation of liver qi into wind

a type of edema especially of the face and head, ascribed to attack on the lung by pathogenic wind, manifested by sudden onset of edema accompanied by fever with aversion to wind, aching joints, and oliguria
a warm disease attributable to contraction of wind-heat pathogen

versions.PMPH: anasarca (general accumulation of serous fluid in various tissues and body cavities)

terms.englishExplanation: a type of distension disease due to contraction of cold pathogen, arising when the pathogen stays in the skin together with the defense qi
an acute febrile disease caused by latent summerheat or summerheat-dampness pathogen, occurring in autumn or winter
a diseased condition arising when qi and blood of the liver meridian become depressed and stagnant
affliction of the lung-superficies by pathogenic wind, mainly manifested as fever, chills, headache, general aching, congested nose, sneezing, itching throat and cough

versions.PMPH: common cold with frightening or convulsion
versions.WFCMS: common cold with frightening or convulsion

terms.englishExplanation: an acute illness characterized by sudden onset of abdominal colic and epigastric oppression accompanied by desire but failure to vomit and desire but failure to defecate

versions.PMPH: chylous strangury (chyle: a milky fluid consisting of lymph and emulsified fat extracted from chyme by the lacteals during digestion and passed to the bloodstream through the thoracic duct)

terms.englishExplanation: painful discharge of turbid, milky urine like rice-water
a type of jaundice characterized by dizziness immediately after meal, and painful fullness in the stomach due to indigestion
a type of impediment disease mainly involving the bones and joints
a pathogenic factor that may cause diseases marked by abdominal lumps, tympanites and ascites, also known as worm toxin
a disease characterized by the severely distended abdomen like a drum accompanied by a somber yellow discoloration of the skin and prominent veins on the abdominal wall
a diseased state characterized by urinary stoppage and vomiting

terms.englishExplanation: syncope due to yang debilitation and excessive cold
malaria with higher chills and lower fever or even no fever, no sweating, and absence of thirst

versions.PMPH: pi syndrome caused by cold-heat complex
① cold shàn ② testicular hardness and pain due to cold
versions.WFCMS: ①    cold abdominal colic ②    testalgia due to cold

terms.englishExplanation: acute abdominal pain due to cold, also called cold induced colic
diarrhea caused by attack of cold on the gastrointestinal tract

versions.PMPH: fox-creeper disease (hú huò bìng, 狐惑病; equivalent to Behcet's syndrome in Western medicine)

terms.englishExplanation: involuntary and frequent discharge of semen without copulation
diarrhea with fecal incontinence

a type of impediment disease mainly involving the flesh

versions.PMPH: accumulations (fixed abdominal masses of defnite shape; jī, 积)
accumulations and gatherings (abdominal masses; jī jù, 积聚)

poor memory; tendency to forget matters, the same as amnesia

a diseased state attributable to accumulation of pathogens (such as heat or cold in combination with retained fluid or phlegm or stagnant food) in the chest and abdomen, often manifested by local rigidity, fullness and tenderness, also the same as chest constriction
sluggish flow of yang qi in the limbs, leading to retention of water and edema
pathogenic qi binding in the yin meridians/channels.
an impediment disease caused by wind-cold-dampness chiefly involving the sinews
severe case of dysentery with utter loss of appetite and vomiting upon eating and drinking
any diseased state marked by neck rigidity, convulsion of the limbs, and even clenched jaw and opisthotonos

versions.PMPH: gatherings (movable abdominal masses of indefinite shape; jù, 聚)
①fainting (loss of consciousness); syncope ②reversal cold of the limbs

a psychotic disorder characterized by mental and physical hyperactivity, disorganization of behavior, and elevation of mood
a type of strangury characterized by recurrent dripping of urine ascribed to taxing fatigue, usually seen in chronic cases

versions.PMPH: ①consumptive malaria  ② chronic malaria ③  malaria with splenomegaly
versions.WFCMS: consumptive malaria; ① chronic malaria ② malaria with splenomegaly

terms.englishExplanation: chronic malaria with mild chills and fever, and with attacks being brought on by fatigue

versions.PMPH: thunder head wind (a type of headache)

terms.englishExplanation: a diseased state characterized by pain with loud noise in the head
a disease characterized by abdominal pain, tenesmus, diarrhea with stool containing mucus and blood
the pathogenic factor that causes cool dryness pattern/syndrome
a collective term for stagnation of qi, blood, damp, fire, phlegm and food, the same as six stagnations

terms.englishExplanation: dribbling urination or complete blockage of urine flow
a type of impediment disease mainly involving the vessels
common cold in summer
involuntary emission of semen during sleep associated with dreaming
a disease characterized by hardening and thickening of the skin, followed by atrophy at the late stage, referring to scleroderma

precordial pain due to spleen disorder

terms.englishExplanation: infrequent passage of dry hardened feces as a consequence of dysfunction of the spleen

versions.PMPH: pi syndrome (pi zhèng, 痞证; localized sensation of blockage occuring in the chest or abdomen with or without palpable lumps, painless upon pressure)

terms.englishExplanation: chronic recurrent headache occurring on one side or one part of the head

versions.PMPH: ① qi syncope ② qi reversal (synonymous with "      ooooooooo
versions.WFCMS: ① qi syncope ② qi reversal

terms.englishExplanation: syncope induced by emotional upset with upward counterflow of qi
strangury due to bladder qi disorders, characterized by painful voiding of urine and lower abdominal distension and fullness

versions.PMPH: fever due to qi deficiency; qi-deficiency fever
fever due to qi constraint; qi-constraint fever

versions.WFCMS: disease of latent summerheat in autumn

terms.englishExplanation: an impediment disease with an acute onset of joint pain accompanied by local redness, swelling and hotness

strangury ascribed to dampness-heat, marked by urgency and frequency of painful urination with acute onset, and accompanied by chills and fever, lumbar pain and cramps, and distension in the lower abdomen

a type of asthma due to retention of heat-phlegm in the lung, marked by dyspnea, wheezing, thick and yellowish expectoration, flushed face, thirst, reddened tongue with yellow slimy coating and rapid slippery pulse

severe colicky pain in the lower abdomen usually accompanied by constipation and ischuria

versions.PMPH: wind damage (a TCM general term for common cold)

versions.PMPH: xiāo kě of the upper jiao

terms.englishExplanation: a wasting-thirst case characterized by polydipsia with excessive drinking
bleeding from the tongue not due to traumatic injury

versions.WFCMS: ① seminal emission ②loss of essence

terms.englishExplanation: malaria complicated by dampness, manifested as paroxysms of chills and unsurfaced fever, accompanied by impaired sweating, nausea, vomiting, anorexia, slimy tongue coating and relaxed pulse

terms.englishExplanation: painful and difficult urination due to the passage of urinary calculi
a type of edema marked by stony hardness of the lower abdomen
a disease attributable to invasion of the lung-superficies by an epidemic pathogen that causes acute fever, sore throat, headache and general aching
epidemic infectious disease in a certain season
dyspnea caused by external contraction, marked by rapid, forceful and coarse breathing with acute onset and short duration

versions.PMPH: pǐ syndrome due to excess, excess pǐ

terms.englishExplanation: sensation of stuffiness caused by accumulation of pathogenic factors
syncope due to eating and drinking too much at one sitting

versions.PMPH: gān (疳) yellowing due to food strain

yin-yang transmission (阴阳易, transmission of remnant heat [of cold-induced or pestilent disease] from one sexual partner to another)

2. stroke; CVA; apoplexy (Western medicine concept)

terms.englishExplanation: a heat pattern/syndrome involving both the exterior and the interior, caused either by heat pathogen entering the interior before the exterior pattern/syndrome is resolved or by superimposition of an exterior pattern/ syndrome of newly contracted warm disease on a preexisting interior pattern/syndrome, usually manifested by fever, aversion to wind, headache, flushed face, thirst, vexation or even delirium, constipation, dark-colored urine, reddened tongue with dry yellow coating and rapid forceful floating pulse, also called the exterior-interior heat pattern/syndrome

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of dual exterior and interior excess
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of dual exterior and interior excess

terms.englishExplanation: an excess pattern/syndrome involving both the exterior and interior, usually manifested by fever without sweating, headache with stiff painful nape, irritability, abdominal distension and constipation

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of dual exterior and interior deficiency
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of dual exterior and interior deficiency

terms.englishExplanation: a deficiency pattern/syndrome involving both the exterior and the interior, usually manifested by sweating with aversion to wind, shortage of qi and reluctance to speak, decreased food intake, loose bowels, pale tongue and weak pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of exterior heat and interior cold
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of exterior heat and interior cold

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome with pre-existing insufficient yang qi complicated by contraction of wind-heat, manifested by fever, slight aversion to cold, headache and generalized pain, cough, loose bowels, pale and flabby tongue with thin yellow coating and rapid floating pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of exterior excess and interior deficiency
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of exterior excess and interior deficiency

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome in which exterior excess and interior deficiency occurring simultaneously, manifested by aversion to cold, fever with absence of sweating, listlessness and lassitude, shortness of breath, anorexia, palpitations, white tongue coating and floating pulse

an exterior pattern/syndrome with fever, aversion to cold, headache and generalized aching, forceful or tight floating pulse and absence of sweating, showing the external part of the body being attacked by cold, yet the patient's defense qi is not damaged

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of exterior deficiency and interior excess
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of exterior deficiency and interior excess

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome in which insufficiency of defense is complicated by pathogens bound in the interior, manifested by symptoms of exterior deficiency such as aversion to wind and spontaneous sweating, together with symptoms of interior excess such as abdominal pain, constipation and yellow thick tongue coating

a type of exterior pattern/syndrome resulting from attack by wind while the body's resistance is lowered, manifested by intolerance of wind, persistent sweating, fever, headache, thin white tongue coating, and floating relaxed pulse

a general term for patterns/syndromes that occur chiefly at the early stage of external contractions affecting the exterior part of the body, characterized by a sudden onset, aversion to cold or to wind, fever, headache, generalized pain, thin tongue coating, and floating pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-dampness assailing the exterior

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome resulting from wind-dampness invading the exterior part of the body, manifested by heavy cumbersome limbs, dizziness with heavy head, aversion to cold, fever not relieved after sweating, oppression in the chest, no desire to drink, white slimy tongue coating and soggy relaxed pulse
a method of pattern identification/syndrome differentiation in which symptoms are categorized as heat or cold, thereby determining the nature of the disease

versions.PMPH: cold dominating heat; cold more pronounced than heat

terms.englishExplanation: a general term for patterns/syndromes caused either by external cold pathogen or by insufficient yang within the body, commonly manifested by aversion to cold or fear of cold, cold pain with preference for heat, absence of thirst, thin clear sputum and nasal mucus, long voidings of clear urine, loose bowels, white facial complexion, pale tongue with white coating, and tight or slow pulse

a pattern/syndrome arising from deficiency of essential qi, marked by emaciation, dizziness, tinnitus, short and slight of stature, slow in movements, low intelligence, or by infertility, sterility, impotence and premature ejaculation

a type of interior pattern/syndrome attributable to deficiency of yang qi of bowels and viscera or external cold directly attacking on or passing to the interior, mainly manifested by intolerance of cold with cold limbs, somber white complexion, absence of thirst or liking for hot drinks, long voidings of clear urine, pale tongue with white moistened coating and slow sunken pulse

a type of interior pattern/syndrome with exuberant heat arising when external pathogens enter the internal organs and transform into heat, mainly manifested by fever with sweating, thirst with intake of fluid, vexation, bitter taste in the mouth, short voiding of reddish urine, a reddened tongue with yellow coating, and rapid surging or rapid string-like pulse

(1) any pattern/syndrome resulting from external pathogen transforming into heat and entering the interior to bind in the stomach and intestines, manifested by high fever, vexing thirst, abdominal pain and constipation; (2) a general term for patterns/ syndromes caused by retention of phlegm, blood stasis, food stagnation or accumulation of parasitic worms

a type of interior pattern/syndrome attributable to deficiency of qi, blood, yin, and yang of the internal organs with diminished function, usually manifested by shortage of qi, reluctance to speak, listlessness, lassitude, palpitations, dizziness, tinnitus, decreased food intake, aching lumbus and limp knees, pale tender-soft tongue and weak pulse
a general term for patterns/syndromes that indicate the existence of disease in the interior of the body such as bowels and viscera, qi and blood, or bone marrow

a general term for patterns/syndromes resulting either from attack of external heat or from prevalence of yang qi, usually manifested by fever, aversion to heat and liking for cold, thirst, flushed face, irritability and vexation, thick yellow sputum and nasal mucus, short voidings of dark-colored urine, constipation, reddened tongue with yellow coating, and rapid pulse

versions.WHO: upper cold and lower heat pattern/ syndrome
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of upper cold and lower heat

terms.englishExplanation: any pattern/syndrome with cold symptoms in the upper part of the body and heat symptoms in the lower

versions.WHO: upper heat and lower cold pattern/ syndrome
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of upper heat and lower cold

terms.englishExplanation: any pattern/syndrome with heat symptoms in the upper part of the body and cold symptoms in the lower

a general term for patterns/syndromes caused by external pathogenic factors such as six excesses, pestilential pathogens, worms and toxins, or by accumulated pathological products due to dysfunction of internal organs, such as phlegm, retained fluid, water, dampness, pus, static blood and retained food

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of summerheat-dampness assailing the exterior

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome resulting from summerheat-dampness attacking the exterior part of the body, manifested by fever, slight aversion to wind and cold, fatigued and cumbersome body, heavy head as if swathed, grimy face, thirst and vexation, reddened tongue with yellow slimy coating and rapid soggy pulse

versions.PMPH: external cold and internal heat syndrome
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of external cold and internal heat

terms.englishExplanation: a serious pattern/syndrome resulting from exhaustion of yin fluid, manifested by thirst and craving for cold drink, flushed face, restlessness, dry tongue and rapid fine pulse

versions.WHO: D eficiency- Excess Pattern Identification/ Syndrome Differentiation

terms.englishExplanation: categorization of pattern identification/syndrome differentiation in which the strength of the healthy qi and pathogenic qi are compared, thereby determining the nature of the disease
a general term for patterns/syndromes caused by deficiency of the healthy qi (including deficiency of yin, yang, qi and blood)

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of detriment to yang affecting yin
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yang impairment affecting yin

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when long-term debilitation of yang qi causes damage to yin, resulting in deficiency of both yin and yang, in which yang deficiency is primary while yin deficiency secondary

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yang deficiency with congealing cold
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency and congealing cold

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by aversion to cold, cold limbs, pain in the chest, epigastrium, abdomen, waist and knees relieved by warmth, and in women, late periods, painful periods, menstrual discharge of dark blood and clots, pale and enlarged tongue with white slippery coating, and slow sunken pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yang deficiency with qi stagnation
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency and qi stagnation

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of qi stagnation arising from yang deficiency, manifested by aversion to cold, cold limbs, pale complexion, painful distention in the chest, epigastrium and abdomen, borborygmi, sloppy stools, long voidings of clear urine, pale and enlarged tongue, and slow, sunken and weak pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yang deficiency with dampness obstruction
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency and dampness obstruction

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of dampness obstruction attributed to insufficient yang qi with disordered qi transformation, manifested by aversion to cold, cumbersome limbs, edema, inhibited urine, sloppy stool, reduced food intake and abdominal distension,

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yang deficiency with water flood
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency and water diffusion

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney leads to retention of water in the body, usually manifested by generalized edema, inhibited urine, palpitation, dyspnea, aversion to cold with cold limbs, abdominal distension, soggy diarrhea, pale and plump tongue with white slippery coating, and slow, sunken and weak pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yang deficiency with congealing phlegm
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency and phlegm coagulation

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of insufficient yang qi complicated with congealing phlegm, manifested by aversion to cold, cold limbs, dizziness, somnolence, oppression in the chest with profuse phlegm, obesity, or goiter, swollen inflamed lymph glands, breast nodules, swollen and stiff joints, slimy tongue coating and slippery pulse

versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency and external contraction

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome resulting from deficiency of yang qi with reduction in its warming and activating power, manifested by intolerance of cold, cold extremities, spontaneous sweating, loose bowel, long voidings of clear urine, pale tongue, and deep and weak pulse; in Kampo medicine, it is understood as “yang pattern in combination with excess pattern”

a collective term for exterior, heat and excess patterns/ syndromes with excitatory, hyperfunctional, restless or bright manifestations, or outward and upward symptoms, as well as morbid conditions caused by pathogenic factors of yang nature

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin exhaustion and yang collapse
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin exhaustion and yang collapse

terms.englishExplanation: a critical pattern/syndrome of yin-essence exhaustion followed by yang collapse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of exuberant yin repelling yang
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of exuberant yin repelling yang

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome in which exuberant internal cold forces yang qi to the outer body, giving rise to signs of true internal cold and false external heat

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of detriment to yin affecting yang
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin impairment affecting yang

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when long-term depletion of yin fluid causes damage to yang, resulting in deficiency of both yin and yang, in which yin deficiency is primary while yang deficiency secondary

terms.pinyin: yīn xū bí qiào shī rú zhèng

versions.PMPH: yin deficiency with loss of nasal moisture

versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and bleeding

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with effulgent fire
versions.PMPH: vigorous fire due to yin deficiency
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and effulgent fire

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of effulgent fire arising from yin fluid deficiency, manifested by vexation, insomnia, dry throat and mouth, night sweating, nocturnal emission flushed cheeks, bone-steaming tidal fever, dry and bound stool, short voidings of yellow urine, or hemoptysis, epistaxis, reddened tongue lacking moisture, or ulceration on the tongue and rapid fine pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with fluid depletion
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and fluid inadequacy

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of yin fluid deficiency, manifested by dire thirst, dry skin, sunken eyes, short voidings of yellow urine, dry and bound stool, vexing heat in the chest, palms and soles, emaciation, night sweats, reddened tongue with scanty coating and rapid fine pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with internal heat
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and internal heat

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome resulting from deficiency of yin fluid with endogenous heat, usually manifested by persistent low fever or afternoon tidal fever, heat sensation in the chest, palms and soles, malar flush, night sweats, dry mouth with desire for drink, short voidings of dark-colored urine, constipation, reddened tongue lacking moisture and rapid fine pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency and dampness-heat
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and dampness-heat

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of yin-fluid deficiency complicated with dampness-heat obstruction, manifested by low fever, night sweating, flushed cheeks in the afternoon, vexing heat in the chest, palms and soles, bitter and greasy taste in the mouth, cumbersome limbs, reddened tongue with yellow slimy coating, and rapid fine pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with water retention
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and water retention

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of yin fluid deficiency complicated with retention of water, manifested by localized edema of limbs, reduced passage of urine, dry throat and mouth, vexing heat in the chest, palms and soles, constipation, reddened tongue with scanty coating and rapid fine pulse

versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and external contraction

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency and blood stasis
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and blood stasis

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of yin fluid deficiency complicated with blood stasis, manifested by vexing heat in the chest, palms and soles, dry throat and mouth, low fever in the afternoon, local stabbing pain, expectoration of dark blood with clots, purple spots on the tongue, and fine rough (choppy) pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with yang hyperactivity
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and yang hyperactivity

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome of yin fluid deficiency with yang hyperactivity, marked by tidal fever, night sweating, flushed cheeks, dizziness, blurred vision, agitation, insomnia, reddened tongue with scanty coating and rapid fine pulse

a pattern/syndrome resulting from deficiency of yin fluid and essence, incapable of restraining yang and usually manifested by emaciation, dizziness, tinnitus, dryness of the mouth and throat, constipation, dark-colored urine, afternoon fever, malar flush, night sweats, reddened tongue with scanty coating and rapid fine pulse; in Kampo medicine, it is understood as “yin pattern in combination with deficiency pattern”

a pattern/syndrome marked by emaciation, sallow complexion, low fever, flushed cheeks, numb limbs, dizziness, blurred vision, palpitations, insomnia, reddened tongue with scanty coating and rapid fine pulse

categorization of patterns/syndromes according to yin-yang theory, e.g., interior, cold, and deficiency patterns/syndromes pertaining to yin, and exterior, heat, and excess patterns/syndromes pertaining to yang

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of dual deficiency of yin and yang
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of both yin and yang deficiency

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome resulting from deficiency of both yin fluid and yang qi of the bowels and viscera, usually manifested as dizziness, tinnitus, lassitude, aversion to cold with cold limbs or vexing heat in the chest, palms or soles, palpitations, lumbar soreness, pale and dry tongue, and rapid weak pulse

a collective term for interior, cold and deficiency patterns/syndromes with inhibitory, hypofunctional, quiescent or dimmed manifestations, or inward and downward symptoms, as well as morbid conditions caused by pathogenic factors of yin nature

versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of true cold with false heat
syndrome/pattern of true heat with false cold
syndrome/pattern of true excess with false deficiency
syndrome/pattern of true deficiency with false excess

terms.pinyin: yīn xū bí qiào shī rú   zhèng

versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and unmoistened nose

terms.chinese: 表寒里热证;外寒里热证
terms.traditional: 表寒裏熱證;外寒裏熱證
terms.pinyin: biǎo hán lǐ rè zhèng ;  wài hán lǐ rè zhèng

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of exterior cold and interior heat

terms.englishExplanation: (1) a pattern/syndrome in which cold pathogen fetters the exterior and depressed heat stays in the interior, mainly manifested by fever without sweat and with aversion to cold, headache and generalized pain, vexation, shortness of breath, fullness of the chest, thirst, reddened tongue with mixed yellow and white coating, and floating tight pulse; (2) a pattern/ syndrome marked by true heat in the interior with external pseudo-cold manifestations

terms.pinyin: biǎo hán zhèng ; fēng hán   shù biǎo zhèng
terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when wind-cold invades the exterior and characterized by pronounced aversion to cold with mild fever, headache, generalized pain, absence of sweating, thin white tongue coating, and tight floating pulse, the same as the pattern/syndrome of wind-cold fettering the exterior

terms.chinese: 表热证;风热犯表证;风热袭表证
terms.traditional: 表熱證;風熱犯表證;風熱襲表證
terms.pinyin: biǎo rè zhèng ;  fēng rè fàn biǎo zhèng ;  fēng rè xí biǎo zhèng
terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when wind-heat invades the exterior and characterized by marked fever with mild aversion to wind and cold, headache, sore throat, sweating and thirst, thin yellow tongue coating and rapid floating pulse, the same as the pattern/ syndrome of wind-heat invading the exterior

any pattern/syndrome characterized by complicated heat and cold conditions such as heat in the upper part with cold in the lower part of the body, cold in the upper part with heat in lower part of the body, heat in the exterior and cold in the interior of the body, or cold in the exterior and heat in the interior of the body

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of clear yang failing to ascend

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by dizziness, blurred vision, tinnitus and impaired hearing, aversion to cold with lack of warmth in the extremities, lassitude and lack of strength, anorexia, sloppy stool, pale tongue with white coating, and relaxed weak pulse
a pattern/syndrome that arises when a heat pathogen congests in the bowels, viscera and meridians/ channels, blocking normal movement out of the body

terms.chinese: 热极生风证;热极动风证
terms.traditional: 熱極生風證;熱極動風證
terms.pinyin: rè jí shēng fēng zhèng ;  rè jí dòng fēng zhèng

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of extreme heat engendering wind

terms.englishExplanation: a liver wind pattern/syndrome attributed to exuberant pathogenic heat, and marked by high fever with restlessness, convulsions, opisthotonos, and impaired consciousness

a pattern/syndrome characterized by damage to yang resulting from various causes such as exuberance of yin-cold in the interior, excessive use of cold-natured medicinals, excessive sweating or diarrhea, usually manifested as fear of cold, pale complexion, spontaneous sweating, cold extremities and faint or even hardly perceptible pulse

a pattern/syndrome characterized by damage to yin, often resulting from invasion of heat pathogen or in advanced cases of febrile disease with impairment of the liver and kidney yin, usually manifested as low fever, heat sensation in the palms and soles, emaciation, thirst, malar flush, dry and scarlet red tongue, and fine, feeble and rapid pulse

versions.WHO: upper exuberance and lower deficiency pattern/syndrome

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by yin deficiency of the liver and kidney in the lower part of the body together with accumulation of qi, static blood or phlegm in the upper part of the body, the same as the excess above and deficiency below pattern/syndrome

terms.pinyin: wáng yáng zhèng ;  yáng tuō zhèng
terms.englishExplanation: a serious pattern/syndrome resulting from exhaustion of yang qi, manifested by pallor, dripping of cold sweats, cold limbs, pale and moistened tongue, and hardly perceptible pulse
a cold pattern/syndrome arising from deficiency of yang qi, the same as the yang deficiency pattern/ syndrome
a heat pattern/syndrome caused by deficiency of yin, qi, blood or fluids
a pattern/syndrome arising from struggle between the healthy and pathogenic qi while excessive pathogens and insufficient healthy qi exist simultaneously

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of yin exuberance with yang debilitation

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when exuberant yin cold leads to debilitation of yang qi, manifested by aversion to cold, cold limbs, long voidings of clear urine, diarrhea, or cold pain with preference for warmth, pale tongue with white coating, and slow sunken pulse
any pattern/syndrome in which yin and yang are out of balance

versions.WHO: true cold with false heat pattern/ syndrome

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when exuberant internal cold forces yang qi to outer body, manifested by subjective feverishness, flushing face, agitation, thirst and sore throat occurring simultaneously with coolness of the chest and abdomen to touch, reversal cold of the limbs, long voidings of clear urine, diarrhea with undigested food, and pale tongue with white coating and sunken string-like pulse

versions.WHO: true heat with false cold pattern/ syndrome

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when exuberant pathogenic heat entraps yang qi in the interior, manifested by cold limbs, aversion to cold or even shivering, impaired consciousness, dark purple complexion, but on the other hand, high fever with the chest and abdomen hot to touch and hot breath exhaled from the mouth and nose, fetid mouth odor, coarse breathing, strong thirst, short voidings of yellow urine, reddened tongue with yellow dry coating and rapid string-like pulse

versions.WHO: true excess with false deficiency pattern/ syndrome

terms.englishExplanation: any pattern/syndrome excess in nature showing some deceptive symptoms of deficiency

versions.WHO: true deficiency with false excess pattern/ syndrome

terms.englishExplanation: any pattern/syndrome deficient in nature showing some deceptive symptoms of excess

versions.WHO: disease cause pattern identification/ syndrome differentiation

terms.englishExplanation: categorization of patterns/syndromes according to pathological conditions attributable to different kinds of causal factors for making diagnosis

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of worm accumulation transforming into malnutrition
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of worm accumulation and malnutrition

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome that arises when intestinal parasites deprive the eyes of nourishment, and that manifests in impaired vision, night blindness, dryness of the eyes and photophobia
a general term for patterns/syndromes caused by intestinal accumulation of parasitic worms, often marked by abdominal distention or pain, emaciation, lack of strength and sallow complexion

versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of toxic fire attacking lip

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome due to wind-toxin attack on the skin and flesh, marked by sudden onset of edema, with numbness, itching and pain, or appearance of wheals, redness and swelling of face, eyes, nose and mouth

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold invading the nose

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by stuffy runny nose or nasal itching with sneezing, pale mucous membrane of the nose with thin clear secretion, associated with aversion to wind and cold, thin white tongue coating and floating tight pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold invading the head

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by headache with pain of the nape and back, aggravated by wind and cold, thin white tongue coating and floating tight pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold assailing the throat

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by painful, itchy and slightly swollen throat dark red in color associated with hoarseness, fever, aversion to cold, absence of sweat, stuffy nose and floating tight pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold assailing the collaterals
versions.PMPH: wind-cold attacking the channels and collaterals
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of wind-cold attacking channel and collateral

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by aversion to cold, pain and cold feeling of the limbs with contracture or numbness and purple or pale discoloration of the skin, white tongue coating and string-like tight pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-fire attacking the eyes

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by reddened, swollen and painful eye and lacrimination associated with fever, aversion to wind and floating rapid pulse

a pattern/syndrome due to accumulation of wind-fire-heat toxin in the skin and flesh, marked by formation of boils, abscess or carbuncle accompanied by itching, numbness and scorching pain or suppuration and ulceration accompanied by high fever, crimson tongue with brownish yellow coating and rapid surging pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-heat invading the nose

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by nasal obstruction and discharge, swollen conchae and congested mucous membrane with thick secretion, impairment of the sense of smell, associated with fever and slight aversion to wind and cold, thin yellow tongue coating and floating rapid pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-heat invading the ear

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by distension and obstruction in the ear, congestion of the tympanic membrane or a feeling of pressure in the ear, associated with tinnitus, headache, fever with slight aversion to wind and cold, thin yellow tongue coating and rapid floating pulse

a pattern/syndrome marked by headache with a distention sensation, fever or aversion to wind, thirst, flushing of face, reddened tongue tip and edges, thin and yellowish tongue coating, and rapid floating pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-heat invading the throat

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by painful swollen throat or enlarged congested tonsils, with discomfort in swallowing and hoarseness of voice, fever, slight aversion to wind and cold, mild thirst, thin yellow tongue coating and rapid floating pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-heat with epidemic toxin
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of wind-heat and epidemic toxin

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by chills and fever, redness and swelling of the head and face, painful swollen throat, skin eruption, agitation, thirst, headache, nausea, crimson tongue and rapid surging pulse

a pattern/syndrome attributed to stagnation of pathogenic wind-cold-dampness (with predominance of wind) in sinews, bones and joints, and manifested by wandering arthralgia with inhibited movement of the joint, thin white tongue coating and floating pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-dampness invading the head

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by headache as if the head were tightly swathed, slight aversion to wind and cold, heaviness feeling of the body and limbs, stuffiness sensation in the chest, anorexia, white slippery tongue coating and soggy pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind-dampness insulting the eyes

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by swelling of the eyelid, reddened and itchy eye with lacrimination, and photophobia

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of wind striking the meridians and collaterals
versions.PMPH: wind striking the channels and collaterals
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of wind striking meridian/channel and collateral

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by numbness, itching or sudden onset of deviation of eye and mouth

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of congealing cold with blood stasis
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of cold congeal and blood stasis

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome arising when pathogenic cold obstructs qi movement and blood flow, manifested by pain aggravated by cold and alleviated by warmth, cold and cyanotic limbs, and delayed menstruation, painful periods, menstrual discharge of dark purple blood with clots, dark purple tongue with white coating, and sunken, slow and choppy pulse

a pattern/syndrome attributed to stagnation of pathogenic wind-cold-dampness (with predominance of cold) in sinews, bones and joints, and manifested by joint cold pain with fixed location, white tongue coating and string-like tight pulse, the same as the pattern/syndrome of cold-prevailing painful arthralgia

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of internal obstruction of cold-dampness
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of internal obstruction of colddampness

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by heaviness feeling of the head and body, joint pain with inhibited bending and stretching, absence of sweat, or edema of the face and limbs, loose stools, and dysuria; white and moistened tongue coating and slippery pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of inward invasion of fire toxin
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of inward invasion of fire toxin

terms.englishExplanation: a syndrome arising when exuberant fire-heat toxin penetrates into the internal organs, usually manifested by high fever with thirst, delirium, constipation, dark-colored urine, crimson tongue with yellow coating and rapid sunken pulse

a pattern/syndrome caused by toxin derived from exuberant heat-fire retaining in the skin and subcutaneous tissue, marked by local redness, swelling and burning pain, followed by abscess formation and accompanied by fever, thirst, reddened tongue with yellow coating, and rapid pulse
any pattern/syndrome of heat and fire, either contracted externally or engendered internally

any pattern/syndrome of dryness due to consumption of body fluids, manifested by emaciation, withered skin, dry throat, parched lips, thirst, oliguria, constipation, furless reddened tongue and rapid fine pulse
a pattern/syndrome marked by purulent discharge from an ulcerative lesion with stinking fetid smell, accompanied by fever, thirst, curdy and slimy tongue coating, and rapid slippery pulse
seven signs indicating a poor prognosis of external lesions, i.e., poor heart, poor liver, poor spleen, poor lung, poor kidney, deteriorated viscera, and exhausted qi and blood

versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of heat toxin attacking throat

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of heat toxin attacking the tongue
versions.WFCMS: syndrome/pattern of heat toxin attacking tongue

terms.englishExplanation: a pattern/syndrome marked by redness, swelling and pain of the tongue or a part of the tongue with impaired lingual movement, fever, thirst and rapid forceful pulse

a pattern/syndrome attributed to stagnation of pathogenic wind-dampness-heat (with predominance of heat) in sinews, bones and joints, and manifested by joint pain with local scorching hotness, redness and swelling, yellow tongue coating and rapid slippery pulse

versions.WHO: pattern/syndrome of cold in the middle

a pattern/syndrome caused by externally contracted wind and fire in combination

a pattern/syndrome caused by externally contracted wind and heat, and manifested by pronounced fever and mild aversion to cold, cough, thirst, reddened tongue margins and tip, slightly yellow coating and rapid floating pulse
a pattern/syndrome caused by externally contracted wind and dampness in combination, manifested by generalized pain, heaviness feeling of the body, joint pain with inhibited articular movement

a pattern/syndrome caused by externally contracted wind and dryness in combination, and manifested by headache, fever, aversion to wind, absence of sweating, dry nose, lips, throat and skin, dry cough, thin and dry tongue coating

The general signs of phlegm-dampness are white slimy tongue coating and slippery pulse

pattern/syndrome caused by exopathic warmth and dryness in autumn, marked by fever with slight aversion to wind and cold, dryness of the nasal cavity and lips, thirst, dry cough with scanty expectoration, fidgetiness, thin yellowish tongue fur, and rapid and floating pulse
